# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build an Elasticsearch snapshot configuration for automated backups. Configure snapshot repository to S3 or file share for the operations team. Implement snapshot creation and restoration functionality.
# Category: datastore_security

version: '1' # Version of your YAML syntax that should be used (can also use `---` if you prefer) 
             # Here it is just a placeholder, replace with actual version as per Elasticsearch documentation and requirements.  
             
kind: ConfigMap               # Name for the Configuration map in Kubernetes cluster/namespace where this config resides          
metadata:                     # Define metadata of your configuration           
    name: es-snapshot          # Provide a unique identifier (Name) to describe what is going on               
    namespace: default         # Specify Namespace  - if it's not already defined then Kubernetes will use the Default one.                 
    
data:                        # Data fields of your configuration     
   es-snapshot.config: |         
        snapshot.enabled: true                     
            type: s3                          // Type can be 'file', or an S3 bucket name, etc      
                region: uswest                      
                    endpoint: http://s3.amazonaws.com/bucket     
                        in_sync_delay: 60m                     # time to wait for the data changes before sending a snapshot   // Value can be duration string like "5h" or an integer value representing seconds         
            replications : '1'                 /* Replication factor, it should match your cluster shards */                 
                delay_before_removal: 240m    # How long to wait before removing snapshots if there are any unrestored data points in the S3 bucket       // Value can be duration string like "5h" or an integer value representing seconds    
            storage.size : '1GB'                 /* Snapshot repository size */  // Here you need specific snapshot file and space   Size is limited by system disk - here, only free memory will count as available volume for storing snapshots        
                s3_bucket: "es-snapshoots"    # Name of the S3 bucket where to store your data (make sure that it exists)  // You should have this in place before running script
